visa_types.name: A/2 Student Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Required documents:
- Travel document valid for three months longer than Visa validity (usually six months). Including all old/ other passports.
- Signed and filled application form.
- Official acceptance letter to an institution recognized by the Council for Higher Education, study programs of the Volcani Centre (agricultural research) and to the B.A. study program in Business Administration at the China-Israel Institute.
- If the student has first-degree dependents - ensure their details and passport numbers appear on the official acceptance letter.
- Proven means of living (bank statements for three months prior to application).
- Birth certificate (not legalized).
- Copies of Parents’ passports to verify applicant isn’t an Israeli citizen.
- Medical insurance for period of study.
- Proof of stay in Israel (minimum first month) in dorms/rental/Air BnB etc.
- Two recent colour passport photos, against a white background, unobstructed face, 5 x 5 cm.
For a minor:
- Consent form filled and signed by both parents/ legal guardian.
- Original birth certificate or other official document with child and parents’ names written.
visa_types.visa_fees:
Fees are due on the visa application when applying.

[Table:  A/2 Student Visa]
Applicants may choose to pay at the application centre using Credit Card or cash .
visa_types.processing_time: Standard visa processing time is 7 working days from the day of receipt of the application at the Embassy. In case the documents are not submitted as per requirements or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Student Visas are granted to students who belong to institutions recognized by the Council for Higher Education, study programs of the Volcani Centre (agricultural research) and to the B.A. study program in Business Administration at the China-Israel Institute.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Student Visas are granted to students who belong to institutions recognized by the Council for Higher Education, study programs of the Volcani Centre (agricultural research) and to the B.A. study program in Business Administration at the China-Israel Institute.

## Visa Fees
Fees are due on the visa application when applying.

[Table:  A/2 Student Visa]
Applicants may choose to pay at the application centre using Credit Card or cash . 

## Documents Required
Required documents:
- Travel document valid for three months longer than Visa validity (usually six months). Including all old/ other passports.
- Signed and filled application form.
- Official acceptance letter to an institution recognized by the Council for Higher Education, study programs of the Volcani Centre (agricultural research) and to the B.A. study program in Business Administration at the China-Israel Institute.
- If the student has first-degree dependents - ensure their details and passport numbers appear on the official acceptance letter.
- Proven means of living (bank statements for three months prior to application).
- Birth certificate (not legalized).
- Copies of Parents’ passports to verify applicant isn’t an Israeli citizen.
- Medical insurance for period of study.
- Proof of stay in Israel (minimum first month) in dorms/rental/Air BnB etc.
- Two recent colour passport photos, against a white background, unobstructed face, 5 x 5 cm.
For a minor:
- Consent form filled and signed by both parents/ legal guardian.
- Original birth certificate or other official document with child and parents’ names written.

## Photo Specifications
Recent colour passport photos, against a white background, unobstructed face, 5.5 x 5.5 cm.

## Processing Time
Standard visa processing time is 7 working days from the day of receipt of the application at the Embassy. In case the documents are not submitted as per requirements or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended.

## Download Forms
Applicants, who wish to obtain an Israel visa application form, can download the requisite form here.
Please note the instructions mentioned below while taking the printout.
Download Visa Applications Forms

Please Note:
- Number of pages: 3
- You can also obtain this form from the visa application centre.

The forms are in PDF format.
You will need an Adobe Acrobat Reader to view and download these forms. Please  click here to download Adobe Acrobat Reader.

form

on the required link.

Save Target As
 
from the list

the form on your computer.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic Visa -UN and International Organization Staff
visa_types.documents_required:
Required documents:
- Filled out and signed official Visa application form.
- Note Verbale from foreign Ministry / Embassy including:
- Applicant’s details:
- Purpose of visit / stay.
- Date of arrival
- Position title
- Details of diplomat the applicant is replacing or, if new, title.
- Period of service in Israel
- Details of accompanying family
- If the partners are not married – they must include a letter from the Foreign Ministry that recognizes their relationship.
- If the request is for employees of: UNRWA, UNESCO, OHCHR, OCHA, UNV, approval is required from official Visa department and the International Organizations Department in Jerusalem. Submit the request to them with all the relevant documents.
- Two recent colour passport photos, against a white background, unobstructed face, 5 x 5 cm.
- In case of a minor who travels with one parent (for a short term or long-term visit) - ensure the trip is approved by both parents. If the parents are not married, ask for the parental consent form signed.
visa_types.visa_fees:
Fees are due on the visa application when applying.

[Table:  Diplomatic Visa -UN and International Organization Staff]
Applicants may choose to pay at the application centre using Credit/ Card or Cash.
visa_types.processing_time: Standard visa processing time is 7 working days from the day of receipt of the application at the Embassy. In case the documents are not submitted as per requirements or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
- Diplomatic Visa is granted in a red UN diplomatic passport, for an official title holder, as requested in Note Verbal from the agency or organization headquarters.
- The Visa granted at the Consulate is multiple entry for three months.
- In Israel, a Visa will be granted for the relevant purpose and rest of stay, in accordance with the Note Verbale and passport validity.
- Diplomatic Visa will also be granted to accompanying family – spouse and children up to 18 who hold national passports.
- After the age of 18, children of UN staff receive B/2 for three months.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
- Diplomatic Visa is granted in a red UN diplomatic passport, for an official title holder, as requested in Note Verbal from the agency or organization headquarters.
- The Visa granted at the Consulate is multiple entry for three months.
- In Israel, a Visa will be granted for the relevant purpose and rest of stay, in accordance with the Note Verbale and passport validity.
- Diplomatic Visa will also be granted to accompanying family – spouse and children up to 18 who hold national passports.
- After the age of 18, children of UN staff receive B/2 for three months.

## Visa Fees
Fees are due on the visa application when applying.

[Table:  Diplomatic Visa -UN and International Organization Staff]
Applicants may choose to pay at the application centre using Credit/ Card or Cash.

## Documents Required
Required documents:
- Filled out and signed official Visa application form.
- Note Verbale from foreign Ministry / Embassy including:
- Applicant’s details:
- Purpose of visit / stay.
- Date of arrival
- Position title
- Details of diplomat the applicant is replacing or, if new, title.
- Period of service in Israel
- Details of accompanying family
- If the partners are not married – they must include a letter from the Foreign Ministry that recognizes their relationship.
- If the request is for employees of: UNRWA, UNESCO, OHCHR, OCHA, UNV, approval is required from official Visa department and the International Organizations Department in Jerusalem. Submit the request to them with all the relevant documents.
- Two recent colour passport photos, against a white background, unobstructed face, 5 x 5 cm.
- In case of a minor who travels with one parent (for a short term or long-term visit) - ensure the trip is approved by both parents. If the parents are not married, ask for the parental consent form signed.

## Photo Specifications
Recent colour passport photos, against a white background, unobstructed face, 5.5 x 5.5 cm.

## Processing Time
Standard visa processing time is 7 working days from the day of receipt of the application at the Embassy. In case the documents are not submitted as per requirements or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended.

## Download Forms
Applicants, who wish to obtain an Israel visa application form, can download the requisite form here.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
- A/4 Visa is granted to spouse and minor children of A/2 Visa recipients, when there is official confirmation of their arrival from the institution.
- Holders of A/4 Visa are not permitted to work in Israel.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
- A/4 Visa is granted to spouse and minor children of A/2 Visa recipients, when there is official confirmation of their arrival from the institution.
- Holders of A/4 Visa are not permitted to work in Israel.

## Visa Fees
Fees are due on the visa application when applying.

[Table:  A/4 -Dependents of A/2 ]
Applicants may choose to pay at the Visa application centre using Credit/ Card or Cash. 

## Documents Required
Required documents:
- Travel document valid for three months longer than Visa validity (usually six months). Including all old/ other passports.
- Filled and signed application form in two copies.
- Copy of application for A/2 Visa, whom the applicant is joining.
- Ensure the acceptance letter / confirmation of the A/2 recipient states the applicants’ details (name and passport number).
- If the applicant is the spouse – marriage certificate (not legalized).
- If the applicant is a child – birth certificate with parents’ names written.
- Two recent colour passport photos, against a white background, unobstructed face, 5x5cm.

## Photo Specifications
Recent colour passport photos, against a white background, unobstructed face, 5.5 x 5.5 cm.

## Processing Time
Standard visa processing time is 7 working days from the day of receipt of the application at the Embassy. In case the documents are not submitted as per requirements or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended.

- Eligible for B/4 Visa Volunteers on a Kibbutz Moshav or Welfare Institute.

- Eligible for B/4 Visa Volunteers on a Kibbutz Moshav or Welfare Institute.

## Documents Required

- Travel document valid for three months longer than Visa validity (usually six months). Including all old/ other passports.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
- From November 11, 2019, the Embassy and the visa center will not accept medical certificates with test results for traveling abroad from applicants. All applicants for a work visa will be required to undergo a medical examination only at the International Clinic in Tashkent.
- This means that on the day of submitting documents at the VFS GLOBAL visa center, applicants will also be booked for an appointment at the International Clinic in Tashkent. An appointment at the clinic is automatically scheduled when the applicant books an appointment at the visa center.
- Applicants must visit the clinic on the same day during the clinic's working hours - from 9:00 to 12:00. The results of the medical examination will be sent directly to the Embassy of Israel in the Republic of Uzbekistan.
- Payment for the medical examination, in the amount of 1,200,000 sum, must be made by applicants to an employee of the International Clinic in Tashkent, who will be at the Embassy of Israel on the day of the interview. The day of the interview is also appointed by the visa center. There is no need to provide any medical certificates from other medical institutions in Uzbekistan.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
- From November 11, 2019, the Embassy and the visa center will not accept medical certificates with test results for traveling abroad from applicants. All applicants for a work visa will be required to undergo a medical examination only at the International Clinic in Tashkent.
- This means that on the day of submitting documents at the VFS GLOBAL visa center, applicants will also be booked for an appointment at the International Clinic in Tashkent. An appointment at the clinic is automatically scheduled when the applicant books an appointment at the visa center.
- Applicants must visit the clinic on the same day during the clinic's working hours - from 9:00 to 12:00. The results of the medical examination will be sent directly to the Embassy of Israel in the Republic of Uzbekistan.
- Payment for the medical examination, in the amount of 1,200,000 sum, must be made by applicants to an employee of the International Clinic in Tashkent, who will be at the Embassy of Israel on the day of the interview. The day of the interview is also appointed by the visa center. There is no need to provide any medical certificates from other medical institutions in Uzbekistan.

## Visa Fees
Fees are due on the visa application when applying.

[Table:  Work visa B/1 for work under a contract.]
Applicants may choose to pay at the application centre using Credit/ Card or Cash.

## Documents Required
Required documents:
To obtain a work visa, client must first find a company or individual in Israel who is willing to invite an employee to work in Israel.
An employer in Israel must contact the Israeli Ministry of Interior and obtain an official work permit for their future employee, which is later forwarded to the Embassy.
After concluding a contract with an employer, the applicant for a work visa must provide the following documents to the Embassy:
- A passport valid for at least 7 months at the time of application
- 2 color photographs, 5.5 x 5.5 cm on a white background;
- A birth certificate with a translation into English, certified by the Apostille seal
- A marriage/divorce/death certificate of a spouse or a certificate from the Civil Registry Office on the absence of family relations (celibacy) with a translation into English, certified by the Apostille seal;
- A certificate of no criminal record with a translation into English, certified by the Apostille seal;
- All applicants for a work visa will have to undergo a medical examination only at the International Clinic in Tashkent.;
- A diploma of education with an appendix. For example, if the applicant is sent to work as a caregiver for the elderly, he must provide a diploma of completed medical education or a diploma of completion of a course in caring for the disabled and the elderly - with a translation into English, certified by the Apostille seal;
- A contract between the employer and the employee, signed by both parties. The employee must sign the contract in the presence of a notary. The contract must be signed by a notary and certified by the Apostille seal.
A certificate of no criminal record, birth certificate, document on marital status, diploma with an appendix must be translated into English and certified by the Apostille seal. The contract must also be certified by the Apostille seal.
After receiving the above documents and an official invitation through the Israeli Ministry of Interior, the embassy will review the visa application.
B/1 visa for persons of Jewish descent
You must contact the Repatriation (Permanent Residence) Department to undergo consular verification.
After receiving permission, you must make an appointment at the visa center and provide the following documents:
- A passport valid for at least 7 months at the time of application
- Provide the original of your birth certificate and a copy;
- 2 color photographs, 5.5 x 5.5 cm on a white background;
- Fill out the application form.
- Medical insurance.
The consular fee is not paid in this case

## Photo Specifications
Recent colour passport photos, against a white background, unobstructed face, 5.5 x 5.5 cm.

## Processing Time
Standard visa processing time is 7 working days from the day of receipt of the application at the Embassy. In case the documents are not submitted as per requirements or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended.

## Download Forms
Applicants, who wish to obtain an Israel visa application form, can download the requisite form here.
Please note the instructions mentioned below while taking the printout.

 (Visa Application Form)

- Number of pages: 1

visa_types.overview:
Overview
- A Diplomatic or Service Visa is granted in a diplomatic or service passport, to official position holders, with a Note Verbale from the applicant's foreign ministry at the local Israeli Consulate.
- The Visa granted at the Consulate is multiple entry, according to the service period (maximum 4 years).
- In Israel, a Visa will be granted for the relevant purpose and rest of stay, in accordance to the Note Verbale and Passport Validity.
- Diplomatic or Service Visa will be granted to family: spouse and children, who hold diplomatic or service passports (children till 24 years old).
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
- A Diplomatic or Service Visa is granted in a diplomatic or service passport, to official position holders, with a Note Verbale from the applicant's foreign ministry at the local Israeli Consulate.
- The Visa granted at the Consulate is multiple entry, according to the service period (maximum 4 years).
- In Israel, a Visa will be granted for the relevant purpose and rest of stay, in accordance to the Note Verbale and Passport Validity.
- Diplomatic or Service Visa will be granted to family: spouse and children, who hold diplomatic or service passports (children till 24 years old).

## Visa Fees
Fees are due on the visa application when applying.

[Table:  Diplomatic/ Service Visa -Foreign Diplomats]
Applicants may choose to pay at the application centre using Credit/  Cash. 

## Documents Required
Required documents:
- Filled out and signed official Visa application form.
- Note Verbale from foreign Ministry / Embassy including:
- Applicant’s details:
- Purpose of visit / stay.
- Date of arrival
- Position title
- Details of diplomat the applicant is replacing or, if new, title.
- Period of service in Israel
- Details of accompanying family
- If the partners are not married – they must include a letter from the Foreign Ministry that recognizes their relationship.
- Two recent colour passport photos, against a white background, unobstructed face, 5 x 5 cm.
- In the case of a minor who travels with one parent (for a short term or long-term visit) - ensure the trip is approved by both parents. If the parents are not married, we will require a signed parental consent form.

## Photo Specifications
Recent colour passport photos, against a white background, unobstructed face, 5.5 x 5.5 cm.

## Processing Time
Standard visa processing time is 7 working days from the day of receipt of the application at the Embassy. In case the documents are not submitted as per requirements or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended.

## Download Forms

- Number of pages: 1

You will need an Adobe Acrobat Reader to view and download these forms. Please  click here to download Adobe Acrobat Reader.

form

- When the form opens, save the form on your
 
computer
.
OR
- Press the right button on
 
your mouse
 
on the required link.
- Select
 
Save Target As
 
from the list
- Save
 
the form on your computer.

visa_types.name: B/2 Tourist/ Visitor Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Required documents:
- Travel document valid for three months longer than Visa validity (usually six months). Including all old/ other passports.
- Filled and signed application document.
- Proof of financial means for visit to Israel - Bank Statements for three months.
- Confirmation of employment.
- Place of stay in Israel (hotel reservation/flat rental/Air BnB).
- Return flight tickets.
- Planned itinerary.
- Medical insurance valid for period of stay.
- In the case of a sport team/artists/festival-goers - invitation letter from the inviter in Israel.
- Two recent colour passport photos, against a white background, unobstructed face, 5 x 5 cm.
For a minor:
- Filled and signed consent form by both parents/legal guardian.
- Original birth certificate or any other official document with child and parents’ names written.
Please note:- All the nationalities mentioned below are required to fill out the Visa Application form given at the end of the list.
Afghanistan, Algeria, Egypt, Indonesia, Jordan, North Korea (Democratic People’s Republic of Korea), Kuwait, Libya, Malaysia, Mauritania, Morocco, Oman, Pakistan, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Somalia, Sudan, Tunisia, and Yemen.
All above nationalities please fill in this Visa Application Form.

Individuals who hold Aliya eligibility document from Jewish Agency/ NATIV.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
- B/2 is granted to applicants requesting to visit Israel for tourism, medical tourism, business meetings or short-term non-academic study.
- B/2 holders are not permitted to work in Israel.
- B/2 is usually single entry for three months from day of granting.
- Citizens of countries who signed a Visa exemption treaty do not need to apply before their visit Their stay will be determined upon arriving in Israel.
- A foreign worker who finished working in Israel may apply for B/2 one year after they leave Israel (even if arrived from a country that does not require a Visa).
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
- B/2 is granted to applicants requesting to visit Israel for tourism, medical tourism, business meetings or short-term non-academic study.
- B/2 holders are not permitted to work in Israel.
- B/2 is usually single entry for three months from day of granting.
- Citizens of countries who signed a Visa exemption treaty do not need to apply before their visit Their stay will be determined upon arriving in Israel.
- A foreign worker who finished working in Israel may apply for B/2 one year after they leave Israel (even if arrived from a country that does not require a Visa).

## Visa Fees
Fees are due on the visa application when applying.

[Table:  B/2 Tourist/ Visitor Visa]
Applicants may choose to pay at the Visa application c enter using Credit Card or cash. 
Exempt from fee payment:
Individuals who hold Aliya eligibility document from Jewish Agency/ NATIV.
Citizens of countries Israel has a treaty with.

## Documents Required
Required documents:
- Travel document valid for three months longer than Visa validity (usually six months). Including all old/ other passports.
- Filled and signed application document.
- Proof of financial means for visit to Israel - Bank Statements for three months.
- Confirmation of employment.
- Place of stay in Israel (hotel reservation/flat rental/Air BnB).
- Return flight tickets.
- Planned itinerary.
- Medical insurance valid for period of stay.
- In the case of a sport team/artists/festival-goers - invitation letter from the inviter in Israel.
- Two recent colour passport photos, against a white background, unobstructed face, 5 x 5 cm.
For a minor:
- Filled and signed consent form by both parents/legal guardian.
- Original birth certificate or any other official document with child and parents’ names written.
Please note:- All the nationalities mentioned below are required to fill out the Visa Application form given at the end of the list.
Afghanistan, Algeria, Egypt, Indonesia, Jordan, North Korea (Democratic People’s Republic of Korea), Kuwait, Libya, Malaysia, Mauritania, Morocco, Oman, Pakistan, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Somalia, Sudan, Tunisia, and Yemen.
All above nationalities please fill in this Visa Application Form.

## Photo Specifications
Recent colour passport photos, against a white background, unobstructed face, 5.5 x 5.5 cm.

## Processing Time
Standard visa processing time is 7 working days from the day of receipt of the application at the Embassy. In case the documents are not submitted as per requirements or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended.

## Download Forms
Applicants, who wish to obtain an Israel visa application form, can download the requisite form here.
Please note the instructions mentioned below while taking the printout.
Download Visa Applications Forms
 (Visa Application Form)
Please Note:
- Number of pages: 3
- You can also obtain this form from the visa application centre.
